text: Walk me through your resume.
Why investment banking?
Why this bank specifically?
Tell me about a deal or transaction you have been following in the news. What makes it interesting?
What do you think makes a successful investment banker?
Tell me about a time you worked on a team under extreme time pressure.
Investment banking requires long hours and significant personal sacrifice. How do you handle stress and maintain your performance?
Describe a time you had to pay close attention to detail to avoid a significant mistake.
Tell me about a time you had to manage multiple projects simultaneously. How did you prioritize?
What other firms are you interviewing with? Where does this firm rank?

material: A client is considering an acquisition of a competitor. During your due diligence, you discover that the target company's revenue projections appear overly optimistic compared to industry benchmarks.
text: How would you raise this concern with the deal team and the client?
Tell me about a time you built a financial model or performed a detailed quantitative analysis. What was the result?
Where do you see yourself in five years within the finance industry?
What industry or sector are you most interested in covering, and why?
Describe a situation where you had to quickly learn a new financial concept or tool to complete an assignment.
Tell me about a leadership experience where you had to motivate a team to meet a tight deadline.

material: You are working on a live deal. At 2 AM, you discover an error in the financial model that could materially change the valuation range presented to the client. The pitch is at 8 AM.
text: Walk me through exactly what you would do in this situation.
What is the most important quality you would bring to our analyst class?
Tell me about a time you disagreed with a supervisor. How did you handle it?
How do you stay informed about financial markets and current events?
If you had to pitch a stock right now, which company would you choose and why?

material: You are a first-year analyst. A Vice President asks you to make a change to a presentation that you believe contains misleading information, though it is not technically false.
text: How would you handle this situation?
Tell me about a time when your integrity was tested.
Tell me about a time you had to persuade someone who initially disagreed with your analysis or recommendation.
Describe a situation where you had to work with incomplete information to make a decision. How did you proceed?
What do you think differentiates a good investment banker from a great one?
Walk me through a recent M&A transaction that caught your attention and explain why the strategic rationale made sense.
Tell me about a time you received critical feedback. How did you respond and what did you change?
Why do you want to work in finance rather than consulting, tech, or another industry?
Describe a time when you had to deliver bad news to a teammate or stakeholder. How did you handle it?
If you were staffed on a live deal and your analyst class had a planned social event, how would you handle the conflict?
Tell me about a time you made a mistake at work or school. What happened and what did you learn?
What is a recent IPO you found interesting, and what would you have advised the company on pricing?
Describe a time you had to adapt quickly to a major change in a project or assignment.
How would you explain a leveraged buyout to someone with no finance background?
Tell me about a time you went above and beyond what was expected of you.
A managing director asks you to send a client presentation in 30 minutes, but you notice a data error on one of the slides. What do you do?
What is a sector or industry you are particularly passionate about and why?
Describe a time you had to balance competing priorities from multiple senior people. How did you manage?
If a client CEO calls and asks for your personal opinion on whether they should pursue an acquisition, how do you respond?
Tell me about a group project where a team member was not pulling their weight. What did you do?
What do you think is the biggest risk facing the global financial markets right now?
Describe a situation where you had to learn something technical very quickly to complete a task.
You discover that a senior banker has been inflating revenue projections in a pitch book to win a mandate. What do you do?
How do you prioritize tasks when everything seems urgent?
Tell me about a time you showed initiative without being asked.
What would you do if you were asked to work on a deal for a company whose business practices you personally disagree with?
Describe a time you had to build a relationship with someone who was initially difficult to work with.
Walk me through a recent restructuring or bankruptcy that you followed. What were the key issues?
If two vice presidents give you conflicting instructions on the same deliverable, how do you handle it?
Tell me about a time you used data or analysis to change someone's mind.
What is one thing about investment banking culture that concerns you, and how do you plan to manage it?
Describe a situation where you had to work with people from different backgrounds or perspectives. What did you learn?
A client asks you to include overly optimistic assumptions in a financial model to make their company look better to potential buyers. How do you respond?
What qualities do you look for in a mentor, and how have mentors shaped your career path so far?
Tell me about a time you failed to meet a deadline. What happened and how did you recover?
How do you think rising interest rates have impacted M&A activity, and what does that mean for our business?
Describe a time you had to communicate a complex idea to a non-technical audience.
You overhear a colleague discussing material non-public information about a client in a public setting. What do you do?
What is the most challenging analytical project you have completed, and what made it difficult?
Tell me about a time you had to say no to someone in a position of authority. How did you handle it?
If you could advise any company in the world on a strategic transaction right now, which would it be and why?
Describe a situation where you identified a problem before anyone else noticed it. What action did you take?
How do you maintain accuracy and attention to detail when you are exhausted?
Tell me about a time you had to make a decision with significant consequences under time pressure.
What is your understanding of Chinese wall policies in investment banking, and why are they important?
Describe a time when you took on a responsibility outside your comfort zone. What was the outcome?
A first-year analyst on your team is visibly struggling and falling behind. How do you approach the situation?
What do you think about the role of ESG considerations in investment banking advisory?
Tell me about a long-term goal you set for yourself and the steps you took to achieve it.
How would you handle a situation where a client's interests conflict with what you believe is the best financial advice?
Describe a time you had to manage upward, keeping a busy superior informed without being a burden.
What recent regulatory change do you think will have the biggest impact on the banking industry?
Tell me about a time you had to quickly build credibility with a new team or group.
You realize after a pitch meeting that one of the comps in your valuation was incorrect. The MD seems happy with how it went. Do you say something?
What is your approach to networking, and how have you built professional relationships in the past?
Describe a time you turned a setback into an opportunity.
How do you think artificial intelligence and automation will change the investment banking industry over the next decade?
Tell me about a time you had to work with someone whose work style was very different from yours.
Walk me through a debt issuance or capital raise you have been following. What made the terms attractive or unattractive?
If you had a free Sunday during a quiet period, how would you use it to develop professionally?
Describe a time when you had to choose between doing something the easy way and doing it the right way.
What do you think is the most undervalued sector in the market today, and what is your thesis?
Tell me about a time you managed a high-stakes relationship where trust was critical.
How would you handle a situation where you are asked to work on a project that conflicts with a personal commitment you have already made?
